AGENTS.md.contentPreview:
# Agent Instructions

## Project Overview

This sample API project demonstrates an agent-readable OpenAPI contract.

## Setup Commands

```bash
npm test
```

```bash
npm run lint
```

```bash
npm run typecheck
```

## Architecture Map

- `openapi.yaml`: API contract.
- `agentlighthouse.tasks.yaml`: agent task benchmark.

## Coding Conventions

Keep examples placeholder-safe and update tests with contract changes.

## Common Mistakes

- Do not use real API keys.
- Do not edit generated build outp

CLAUDE.md.contentPreview:
# Claude Memory

OpenAPI Good Project is a sandbox API contract fixture for AgentLighthouse.

## Workflow

- Run `npm test`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run typecheck`.
- Keep `openapi.yaml` examples placeholder-safe.

## Boundaries

Do not add real credentials. Destructive API examples must remain sandbox-only.

# OpenAPI Good Project

## Quickstart

Install dependencies and verify the API contract:

```bash
npm test
```

## Usage Example

Use `GET /v1/customers` to list customers and `POST /v1/customers` to create one with an idempotency key.

## Architecture

- `openapi.yaml` contains the API contract.
- `agentlighthouse.tasks.yaml` contains verifiable agent workflows.
- `AGENTS.md` contains agent operating instructions.

## Troubleshooting

If authentication fails, check that `EXAMPLE_API_KEY` is rep

textByPath.AGENTS.md:
# Agent Instructions

## Project Overview

This sample API project demonstrates an agent-readable OpenAPI contract.

## Setup Commands

```bash
npm test
```

```bash
npm run lint
```

```bash
npm run typecheck
```

## Architecture Map

- `openapi.yaml`: API contract.
- `agentlighthouse.tasks.yaml`: agent task benchmark.

## Coding Conventions

Keep examples placeholder-safe and update tests with contract changes.

## Common Mistakes

- Do not use real API keys.
- Do not edit generated build output.
- Do not remove error responses when simplifying examples.

## Security and Privacy

Never use real API keys. Use `EXAMPLE_API_KEY` in examples.

## Ownership

API contract changes require maintainer review.
textByPath.CLAUDE.md:
# Claude Memory

OpenAPI Good Project is a sandbox API contract fixture for AgentLighthouse.

## Workflow

- Run `npm test`, `npm run lint`, and `npm run typecheck`.
- Keep `openapi.yaml` examples placeholder-safe.

## Boundaries

Do not add real credentials. Destructive API examples must remain sandbox-only.
textByPath.README.md:
# OpenAPI Good Project

## Quickstart

Install dependencies and verify the API contract:

```bash
npm test
```

## Usage Example

Use `GET /v1/customers` to list customers and `POST /v1/customers` to create one with an idempotency key.

## Architecture

- `openapi.yaml` contains the API contract.
- `agentlighthouse.tasks.yaml` contains verifiable agent workflows.
- `AGENTS.md` contains agent operating instructions.

## Troubleshooting

If authentication fails, check that `EXAMPLE_API_KEY` is replaced by a sandbox key.
textByPath.agentlighthouse.tasks.yaml:
version: 0.1.0
project: openapi-good-project
tasks:
  - id: add-customer-api-example
    title: Add a customer API example
    persona: SDK maintainer
    objective: Add a runnable customer creation example that uses the documented idempotency key and sandbox API key placeholder.
    projectAreas:
      - openapi
      - docs
    requiredDocs:
      - README.md
      - openapi.yaml
      - AGENTS.md
    allowedFiles:
      - README.md
      - openapi.yaml
    disallowedFiles:
      - .env
    expectedActions:
      - Read the OpenAPI request body schema.
      - Preserve placeholder credentials.
    expectedOutputs:
      - A request example with idempotency_key and email.
    successCriteria:
      - Example uses EXAMPLE_API_KEY.
      - Example includes idempotency_key.
    verificationCommands:
      - npm test
    riskLevel: medium
    commonFailureModes:
      - Omits Authorization header.
      - Uses a realistic-looking API key.
textByPath.docs/ARCHITECTURE.md:
# Architecture

- `openapi.yaml` defines the API contract.
- `agentlighthouse.tasks.yaml` defines verifiable agent workflows.
- `AGENTS.md` defines coding-agent operating rules.

The fixture is intentionally small but should remain semantically strong for API analysis.
textByPath.openapi.yaml:
openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  title: Customer Platform API
  version: 1.0.0
  description: Customer Platform API for listing, creating, and deleting sandbox customers with explicit authentication, idempotency, pagination, and error recovery guidance for generated clients.
servers:
  - url: https://api.example.com/v1
security:
  - bearerAuth: []
tags:
  - name: Customers
    description: Customer account workflows.
externalDocs:
  url: https://docs.example.com
components:
  securitySchemes:
    bearerAuth:
      type: http
      scheme: bearer
  schemas:
    CustomerCreate:
      type: object
      required: [email, idempotency_key]
      properties:
        email:
          type: string
          description: Customer email address in the sandbox workspace.
        idempotency_key:
          type: string
          description: Unique key that prevents duplicate customer creation on retries.
    Customer:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
          description: Stable customer identifier.
        email:
          type: string
          description: Customer email address.
paths:
  /customers:
    get:
      operationId: listCustomers
      summary: List customers
      description: Lists sandbox customers using cursor pagination. Use limit and cursor to continue through all pages. A 429 response includes Retry-After guidance.
      parameters:
        - name: limit
          in: query
          description: Maximum number of customers to return, up to 100.
          schema:
            type: integer
        - name: cursor
          in: query
          description: Cursor returned from the previous page.
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        "200":
          description: A paginated list of customers.
          content:
            application/json:
              examples:
                page:
                  value:
                    data: [{ id: cus_123, email: user@example.com }]
                    next_cursor: cursor_456
        "401":
          description: Missing or invalid bearer token.
        "429":
          description: Too many requests; retry after the Retry-After header.
    post:
      operationId: createCustomer
      summary: Create customer
      description: Creates one sandbox customer. This write operation is idempotent when idempotency_key is reused after a network failure.
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/CustomerCreate"
            examples:
              basic:
                value:
                  email: user@example.com
                  idempotency_key: idem_123
      responses:
        "201":
          description: Customer created.
          content:
            application/json:
              examples:
                created:
                  value:
                    id: cus_123
                    email: user@example.com
        "400":
          description: Invalid request body.
        "401":
          description: Missing or invalid bearer token.
        "409":
          description: Idempotency conflict.
        "429":
          description: Too many requests; retry after the Retry-After header.
  /customers/{customer_id}:
    delete:
      operationId: deleteCustomer
      summary: Delete sandbox customer
      description: Destructive sandbox-only operation. Requires maintainer permission and cannot be undone; do not call against production data.
      parameters:
        - name: customer_id
          in: path
          required: true
          description: Stable customer identifier to delete.
          schema:
            type: string
      responses:
        "204":
          description: Customer deleted.
          content:
            application/json:
              examples:
                deleted:
                  value:
                    deleted: true
        "401":
          description: Missing or invalid bearer token.
        "403":
          description: Token lacks delete permission.
        "404":
          description: Customer was not found.
